company_overview.description_of_products_and_services: Baobab Group is a leading digital financial services group focused on serving individuals, micro- and small enterprises in Africa and China. Products include microloans, SME loans, savings accounts, payment services, and mobile money. The group operates primarily as a regulated microfinance institution and digital bank.

anomalies_and_risks.description: PAR>30 ratio increased from 4.0% (2022) to 3.7% (2023) but rebounded to 3.9% (2024). While the GNPA ratio improved slightly (3.0% → 2.8% → 2.9%), absolute NPL volume grew 38% from EUR 47.7m to EUR 65.8m over the period, driven by rapid portfolio expansion. Provision coverage (provisions/gross loans) declined from 3.51% to 3.24%, suggesting the pace of provisioning has not kept up with loan book growth.
anomalies_and_risks.valuation_impact: Potential under-provisioning of EUR 8-12m could require immediate capital injection post-acquisition. Apply -3% to -5% EV adjustment.
anomalies_and_risks.negotiation_leverage: Demand a detailed NPL aging analysis by country. Negotiate an escrow holdback of EUR 15m to cover potential provision shortfall in the first 12 months post-close.

anomalies_and_risks.description: CAR (Tier 1) has steadily declined from 16.01% (2022) to 15.14% (2023) to 14.44% (2024), while RWA grew 39% over the same period. The buffer above the typical 10-12% regulatory minimum is compressing. At current trajectory, CAR could breach 12% by 2026 without a capital raise.
anomalies_and_risks.valuation_impact: May require a EUR 50-80m equity injection within 18-24 months to maintain regulatory buffers. Factor into acquisition price as deferred capital cost.
anomalies_and_risks.negotiation_leverage: Use as leverage to negotiate a lower entry valuation. Request stress-test results showing capital adequacy under adverse scenarios.

anomalies_and_risks.description: Revenue per employee declined from EUR 52,660 (2022) to EUR 55,930 (2023) to EUR 58,170 (2024), while the number of employees grew 26%. Branch count increased 18% (284→335) but revenue per branch only grew 13%. This suggests diminishing marginal returns from new branch openings, particularly in Central Africa where revenue per branch is 40% below the West Africa average.
anomalies_and_risks.valuation_impact: Operational inefficiency in new markets (Central Africa, DRC) may require 2-3 years to reach breakeven. Estimated -2% EV adjustment for sub-scale operations.
anomalies_and_risks.negotiation_leverage: Request country-level P&L breakdown. Negotiate earn-out clauses tied to Central Africa profitability milestones.

anomalies_and_risks.description: The group operates across 9 jurisdictions with varying microfinance regulations. DRC and Nigeria in particular have introduced stricter digital lending regulations in 2024. The company's growing digital disbursement channel (estimated 45% of disbursals) may face additional compliance requirements including rate caps and mandatory cooling-off periods.
anomalies_and_risks.valuation_impact: Regulatory changes in DRC and Nigeria could reduce revenue by EUR 5-10m annually if rate caps are implemented. Risk of fines for non-compliance.
anomalies_and_risks.negotiation_leverage: Demand representations and warranties covering pending regulatory changes. Include a Material Adverse Change clause specifically for regulatory actions in key markets.

anomalies_and_risks.description: While top-line revenue grew 40% (EUR 285m → EUR 399m) from 2022-2024, operating expenses grew 36% (EUR 213m → EUR 290m). The cost-to-income ratio improved only marginally from 74.7% to 72.8%. EBITDA margin expanded from 25.3% to 27.3%, but this is below best-in-class microfinance peers (30-35%). The group's profitability improvement is largely driven by NIM expansion rather than cost optimization.
anomalies_and_risks.valuation_impact: Cost-to-income ratio suggests EUR 15-20m in potential cost synergies available to an acquirer with shared infrastructure. Positive for acquisition thesis.
anomalies_and_risks.negotiation_leverage: Build a detailed cost synergy model to support a higher offer price while demonstrating value creation potential.

anomalies_and_risks.description: Geographic concentration: West Africa accounts for ~49% of total revenue. Senegal and Ivory Coast alone contribute an estimated 35% of group revenue. CFA Franc depreciation risk is significant as these countries share a currency pegged to EUR. China operations (included in 'North Africa & China' segment) show flat growth and may be a strategic misfit in an Africa-focused portfolio.
anomalies_and_risks.valuation_impact: CFA zone concentration amplifies FX and political risk. A 10% CFA devaluation would reduce EUR-reported revenue by ~5%. China operations may have negative strategic value for an Africa-focused acquirer.
anomalies_and_risks.negotiation_leverage: Propose carving out China operations pre-close or pricing them at book value. Negotiate FX hedging requirements into the SPA.

anomalies_and_risks.description: The founding entity Baobab SAS holds 48.2% ownership, giving it effective control. Board composition and related-party transaction disclosures should be scrutinized. Management team tenure data is limited in public filings — CTO and CRO are relatively recent appointments (since 2023), raising questions about institutional continuity during a potential acquisition transition.
anomalies_and_risks.valuation_impact: Key-person risk and controlling shareholder dynamics may affect post-acquisition integration. Budget for retention packages and transition costs (EUR 3-5m).
anomalies_and_risks.negotiation_leverage: Demand tag-along rights and anti-dilution protections. Negotiate management retention agreements as a condition precedent.

core_banking_systems: In-house legacy CBS (still in use at 3 subsidiaries)
quality_of_it.digital_channel_adoption: Mobile banking penetration estimated at 62% of active customers. Digital disbursements account for approximately 45% of total loan volume. WhatsApp chatbot launched in 2024 for customer service.
system_upgrades: Temenos T24 core banking migration (Phase 2 — Senegal, Ivory Coast completed in 2024; remaining markets in 2025)
Cloud migration to Google Cloud Platform initiated in Q3 2024
New fraud detection engine deployed using ML models (2023)
cyber_incidents: No major disclosed cybersecurity incidents. Annual penetration testing conducted by KPMG.

macroeconomic_geo_view.financial_inclusion_rate: 56% of adults with account (2024 World Bank)

competitive_position.market_share_data: Baobab Group is the largest microfinance group in West Africa by gross loan portfolio. In Senegal, it holds an estimated 18-22% share of the regulated microfinance market. In Ivory Coast, market share is approximately 12-15%. The group is a top-3 digital lender in Nigeria by disbursement volume.
competitive_position.central_bank_sector_reports_summary: BCEAO (Central Bank of West African States) 2024 report cites Baobab subsidiaries as 'Category 1 – Systemic' microfinance institutions in Senegal and Ivory Coast. The CBN (Central Bank of Nigeria) digital lending report references Baobab Nigeria as a compliant participant.
competitive_position.industry_studies_summary: CGAP 2024 study ranks Baobab in the top 5 African microfinance groups by digital transformation maturity. MIX Market data shows the group's operational self-sufficiency above 120% consistently since 2020.
competitive_position.customer_growth_or_attrition_news: Active borrower base grew 37.6% from 1.25M (2022) to 1.72M (2024). Mobile app MAU reportedly exceeded 800,000 in Q4 2024. Customer acquisition cost declined 15% year-over-year due to digital channel adoption.

management_quality.name: Arnaud Ventura
management_quality.previous_experience: 20+ years in financial inclusion. Founded Microcred (now Baobab) in 2005 with PlaNet Finance backing. Prior: Investment banking at BNP Paribas (Paris). Built the group from a single-country MFI to a pan-African digital banking group.
management_quality.tenure_history: CEO since founding in 2005 (19+ years). One of the longest-tenured microfinance CEOs in Africa.

management_quality.name: Hélène Boillot
management_quality.previous_experience: 15+ years in finance across banking and audit. VP Finance at Société Générale Africa division (2015-2019). Senior Auditor at Deloitte Paris (2008-2015).

management_quality.name: Aissatou Diallo
management_quality.previous_experience: 12+ years in technology and engineering. Head of Engineering at Wave (mobile money fintech, 2019-2023). R&D Engineer at Ericsson Africa (2013-2019).
management_quality.tenure_history: CTO since 2023 (1+ years). Relatively recent appointment.

management_quality.name: Jean-Marc Lefèvre
management_quality.previous_experience: 18+ years in credit risk and analytics. Head of Credit Risk at BPCE / Groupe Caisse d'Epargne (2016-2023). Senior Risk Analyst at Moody's Analytics (2010-2016).
management_quality.tenure_history: CRO since 2023 (1+ years). Relatively recent appointment.
